description_long: This cowshed is a single-story agricultural building with a broad, overhanging hip roof covered in reddish-brown clay tiles. The structure features a large open central area, likely for livestock, surrounded by a raised platform and partially enclosed walls. The front side includes a prominent staircase leading up to a covered porch supported by columns, providing sheltered access. The roof has a central raised section, possibly for ventilation or additional light, and the eaves extend well beyond the walls for weather protection. The exterior walls appear to be constructed from a combination of brick and concrete, with open bays for airflow. The overall design is functional, with a focus on durability and ease of access for both animals and caretakers.
description: A single-story cowshed with a wide, red-tiled hip roof, open bays, and a central raised section for ventilation. The structure features a front staircase, covered porch, and robust brick and concrete construction.
description_view_0: The front view shows the wide staircase, covered porch, and the main entrance area beneath the overhanging roof.
description_view_1: This side view highlights the depth of the roof eaves and the open bays along the side, with the central raised roof section visible.
description_view_2: The rear view reveals the back platform and the open structure of the cowshed, with the raised roof section and partial enclosure.
description_view_3: The opposite side view shows the open bays and the extended roof, with less detail on the entrance and more focus on the building's length.
attribute_roof_type: hip roof with central raised section
attribute_door_style: single wooden plank door, possibly sliding or hinged, located at the front entrance
attribute_window_presence: no traditional windows; open bays along the sides for ventilation
attribute_ventilation_features: open eaves, central raised roof section for ventilation
